sections.paragraph:
ἐπαύριον (s. αὔριον) adv. ‘tomorrow’ (Polyb. 8, 15, 6; PLille 15, 2 [242 b.c.]; PTebt 119, 17; LXX; on the sp. ἐφ- s. deStrycker, Protevangile, p. 230) in our lit. almost exclusively (as Polyb. 3, 53, 6; PHamb 27, 4 [III b.c.] and mostly LXX; TestSol; TestIss 3:4; GrBar 9:3) τῇ ἐ. (sc. ἡμέρᾳ) on the next day Mt 27:62; Mk 11:12; J 1:29, 35, 43; 6:22; 12:12; Ac 10:9, 23f; 14:20; 20:7; 21:8; 22:30; 23:32; 25:6, 23; GJs 5:1; AcPl Ha 7, 12. εἰς τὴν ἐ. (Polyb. 8, 13, 6) until the next day Ac 4:3 v.l.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

PLille PLille = Papyrus grecs de Lille—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. sp. = spelling

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

PHamb PHamb = Griechische Papyrusurkunden der Hamburger Staats- und Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 360
